subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics leaned on independent sources (IMF Art IV, World Bank, EBRD, V-Dem, RSF, TI, Freedom House, HRW, Eurasianet, RFE/RL); Kazakh state/state-aligned outlets used only for neutral demographic/disaster facts, with reform claims discounted against independent reporting.

Economic figures are IMF/EBRD-validated. Two notable updates: inflation is higher and stickier (~10-12%, a base rate ~18%) than older priors, and the oil-export pipeline chokepoint risk materialised via 2025 drone strikes.

Standout drags: entrenched authoritarian institutions, weak rule of law/press, sticky inflation, a single-point oil-transit dependence on Russia, and severe water degradation. Offsets: fiscal buffers and young demographics.

economic.notes: Fiscal buffers and a strong commodity base, against sticky inflation, limited monetary autonomy, and a resource-curse/transit-chokepoint dynamic.

fiscal_state.notes: Low public debt and a large oil-funded National Fund buffer, against a wide non-oil deficit, an eroding fund, and oil-revenue dependence. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: Limited real autonomy - the base rate forced to ~18% to chase inflation, with an oil-/ruble-sensitive tenge. (IMF; National Bank)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The tenge holds no reserve status. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Elevated and sticky (~10-12%), worsened by a VAT hike. (IMF; NBK)

banking_stability.notes: Cleaned up post-2010s and broadly sound per the IMF, with some opacity. (IMF Art IV)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A strong commodity base (oil/gas, world-leading uranium, minerals) but a classic resource curse, with the main oil-export pipeline a Russia-transit chokepoint repeatedly hit in 2025. (Eurasianet; Caspian Policy)

institutional.notes: IMF-usable macro data, against an authoritarian rule of law and state-controlled media.

rule_of_law.notes: Authoritarian, with no judicial independence; post-2022 'reforms' largely cosmetic and the anti-corruption body folded into the security apparatus. (Freedom House 2026; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Macro data is IMF/EBRD-usable; political and sovereign-fund accounts are opaque. (IMF; TI)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Capable, oil-funded technocratic pockets, but clientelist. (IMF; Freedom House)

press_freedom.notes: Low - state/oligarch media control and jailed or harassed journalists. (RSF 2026; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Young demographics, against suppressed authoritarian stability and restricted liberties.

political_stability.notes: Managed authoritarian calm after the violent January-2022 unrest - suppressed rather than resolved - with a succession/repression tail. (Freedom House; Eurasianet)

civil_liberties.notes: Restricted assembly and opposition, NGO curbs, and online prosecution. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful-ish work/residence routes (it absorbed Russians in 2022), but an authoritarian-lite ceiling with no path to belonging. (HRW; Eurasianet)

social_cohesion.notes: Managed, with ethnic-Russian-north and Russia-influence sensitivities and inequality. (Freedom House)

demographics.notes: A young, growing population with strong natural increase. (UNFPA)

geopolitical.notes: A pragmatic multi-vector hedger, against acute Russia-dependence and transit-spillover exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: A careful multi-vector hedger - Russia, China, and the West - pragmatic but Russia-constrained. (Carnegie; Eurasianet)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct war but acute Russia-dependence/spillover - the oil-export route hit by drones, sanctions-circumvention scrutiny, and a long Russia/China border. (Eurasianet; Carnegie)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Unsanctioned but under circumvention scrutiny, with a managed capital account. (IMF; US Treasury)

physical_practical.notes: Oil-funded infrastructure gains, against severe water/climate degradation and landlocked export vulnerability.

climate.notes: Aridification, Aral/Balkhash water decline, heat and extreme cold, agricultural exposure; oil-funded but middling readiness. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: A Soviet-legacy system, uneven and slowly improving. (World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Oil-funded gains, but landlocked over vast distances with export routes the core vulnerability. (IMF; Eurasianet)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against flood and southern seismic exposure. (ReliefWeb)

summary: A resource-rich economy with fiscal buffers (a large oil-funded sovereign fund), a strong commodity base in oil, gas, uranium, and minerals, and young demographics - offset by entrenched authoritarian institutions with no judicial independence, state-controlled media, sticky double-digit inflation with limited monetary autonomy, and a classic resource-curse dynamic. Its core external vulnerability is a single-point dependence on a Russia-transit oil-export pipeline that was repeatedly struck in 2025, atop severe water and climate degradation and landlocked geography.
